cam_imu_extrinsics.T_cam_imu_translation_meaning: translation FROM imu0 TO cam0 (in cam0 frame)

wrong_distortion_model.vendor_was: radtan, badly fit on >170 deg fisheye
wrong_distortion_model.now: double_sphere — canonical model for fisheye lenses with FOV > 170 deg

no_coverage_qa.now: 96% coverage on 32x18 grid (553/576 cells), heatmap committed

hardcoded_translation.vendor_was: T_cam_imu translation hardcoded, rotation residual not addressed
hardcoded_translation.now: translation solved by Kalibr ([-35, 59, -169] mm, physically matches mount). Rotation verified by gravity decomposition (|g|=9.80).

open_items_phase_g: Repeatability: N=1 capture per protocol. v8 (paper) vs v9 (rigid) showed data-quality matters more than expected. Need v10 with v9-identical protocol to confirm <5mm translation / <0.5ms timeshift drift.
Per-unit study: this calibration is one device. 1000-unit production needs fleet-wide validation OR per-unit jig.

Production binary path: calibration used /home/pi-1/hundred-million-clients/src/recorder; production uses headspace-recorder.service. 30-sec sync test needed to confirm SensorTimestamp deltas <200us between paths.
VIO end-to-end: residuals look right but no full VIO pipeline regression test yet.
IMU intrinsics assumed ideal (no axis misalignment, no scale).
Temperature drift not characterized (calibration at room temp; Pi gets hot under H.264 load).
Kalibr PDF report missing (xvfb-run regen needed).
